_comment: NPC: Temple Priest Liu (庙祝刘). INFERRED from canon context: The Teng family has a temple district for ancestor worship. In Chinese xianxia culture, temples serve as spiritual centers where the community honors ancestors. A priest provides the player with lore about the Teng family's history and foreshadows their downfall.. Derivation: I = INFERRED. Per Article XV, this NPC fills a role implied by canon but not explicitly named.
name: Temple Priest Liu
note: INFERRED: The Teng family has a temple district for ancestor worship. In Chinese xianxia culture, temples serve as spiritual centers where the community honors ancestors. A priest provides the player with lore about the Teng family's history and foreshadows their downfall.

dao_heart.note: 0-100. A cracked Dao heart blocks breakthrough regardless of Qi.

initiation_lines: You come to the temple? Most visitors seek fortune, not wisdom. But you are welcome regardless. The ancestors judge intent, not cultivation.
The Teng family built this temple three generations ago, when they first rose to power. The founder was... a different man from the current patriarch.
I have read the ancestral tablets. There are warnings inscribed on the oldest ones. Warnings about arrogance and the heavens. The current family does not read them.
A young cultivator visited last month. He asked about the restrictions beneath the city. I told him nothing — some knowledge is too dangerous for the living.

sect_tasks.description: Priest Liu sighs: 'The temple needs repairs. The family has neglected it for years. Bring me planks and glass to fix the ancestral shrine. The ancestors deserve better than a crumbling roof.'
Priest Liu nods: 'I need coal for the incense braziers and wheat for the offering bread. The ancestors must not be forgotten — even if the Teng family has forgotten their own teachings.'
